title: Causal Identification and Posterior-Predictive Shields for Provably Safe Adaptive Trading

reviewers.name: Yuan Cheng
reviewers.key_strength: PAC-Bayes Posterior Shield Soundness Theorem with Catoni-style bound elegantly bridges Bayesian beliefs and frequentist safety certificates.
reviewers.key_weakness: Coupled EM inference convergence to identifiable fixed points lacks formal guarantees against spurious fixed points in the nonparametric ANM setting.

reviewers.name: Aniruddha Sinha
reviewers.key_strength: O(K^2*|A|*H*poly(|S|)) verification complexity with convexity-based reduction to polytope vertex checking is a genuine and useful tractability result.
reviewers.key_weakness: Shield liveness guarantee assumes posterior credible set contains a feasible model in all reachable states, which may fail during market crises.

reviewers.name: Lila Zhang
reviewers.key_strength: The 25K LoC Lean 4 formalization commitment mechanizing PAC-Bayes bounds, shield liveness, and composition theorems would be a landmark in formal financial methods.
reviewers.key_weakness: The gap between Lean 4 proofs over idealized real arithmetic and the floating-point Python implementation is unaddressed, weakening the end-to-end guarantee chain.

reviewers.name: Sara Roy
reviewers.key_strength: ML produces the model over which verification operates (rather than being verified itself), creating a mature and practical integration pattern.
reviewers.key_weakness: Evaluation on a single instrument (E-mini S&P 500) limits generalization claims across asset classes and regime structures.

reviewers.name: Joseph S. Chang
reviewers.key_strength: The Composition Theorem's honest presentation as a union bound with novel interface conditions demonstrates intellectual rigor and correct identification of the genuine contribution.
reviewers.key_weakness: Conditional independence between causal-identification failure and shield-verification failure is the load-bearing assumption but its formal justification is underspecified.

consensus_strengths: Regime-Indexed SCMs with ANM structural equations and HSIC-based CI tests provide a principled nonlinear causal framework that goes beyond linear-Gaussian ICP
The PAC-Bayes shield soundness theorem bridges Bayesian and frequentist reasoning with a concrete, tightening bound
The 25K LoC Lean 4 mechanization commitment makes the formal guarantees independently verifiable
Evaluation plan is unusually thorough with model-misspecification experiments, adversarial scenarios, and comprehensive ablations
consensus_weaknesses: Gap between Lean 4 proofs (real arithmetic) and Python implementation (floating-point) is not formally bridged
Coupled EM inference for regime-causal identification has no convergence guarantee against spurious fixed points
State abstraction quality (|S| approximately 1,000) may be insufficient for adversarial or crisis market conditions, and the 0.024 soundness gap is measured on limited scenarios
Single-instrument evaluation (ES futures) limits generalizability claims
Shield liveness theorem's key assumption (feasible model exists in posterior for all reachable states) may fail precisely when safety guarantees are most needed
actionable_recommendations: Bridge the Lean 4 to implementation gap with verified floating-point interval arithmetic or code extraction from Lean 4 to executable
Provide formal convergence analysis for the coupled EM inference showing fixed-point identifiability under stated assumptions, or add a diagnostic that detects convergence to spurious fixed points
Extend evaluation to at least 2-3 additional instruments across different asset classes to validate regime structure generality
Stress-test shield liveness under extreme posterior uncertainty conditions and document graceful degradation behavior
Analyze LASSO pre-selection's interaction with nonlinear ANM causal discovery to ensure causally relevant nonlinear features are not discarded
